events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen guitarist Brian May, CBE, was born on this day in 1947 in Hampton, Middlesex, England.

angles.name: Guitar Hero
angles.copy: The legendary Queen guitarist Brian May celebrates his 79th birthday today. What's your favourite May riff or solo?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: Sir Michael Philip Jagger, lead singer of The Rolling Stones, was born on this day in 1943 in Dartford, Kent, England.

angles.name: Rock & Roll Icon
angles.copy: Still rocking stages worldwide, Mick Jagger celebrates his 83rd birthday today! What's your ultimate Stones track?

events.title: MTV launched with 'Video Killed The Radio Star'
events.blurb: Music Television (MTV) launched on this day in 1981, with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' being the very first music video aired.

angles.name: Changing The Game
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV hit the airwaves, forever changing the music landscape. The first video? The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star'. Did it?

events.title: Oasis released 'Definitely Maybe'
events.blurb: Oasis released their debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on this day in 1994, which became the fastest-selling debut album in British history at the time.

angles.name: Britpop Classic
angles.copy: A quarter-century ago today, Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe'. The album that kicked off a phenomenon. What's your favourite track from it?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, billed as 'An Aquarian Exposition: 3 Days of Peace & Music', began on this day in 1969 in Bethel, New York.

angles.name: Peace & Music
angles.copy: On this day in 1969, half a million people descended on a dairy farm for three days of 'Peace & Music' – the iconic Woodstock Festival. What a moment in history.

events.title: Remembering Elvis Presley
events.blurb: On this day in 1977, the music world lost 'The King of Rock and Roll', Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 this year.

angles.name: The King's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering 'The King', Elvis Presley, who passed away on this day in 1977. His impact on music is immeasurable. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Remembering Tupac Shakur
events.blurb: On this day in 1996, influential rapper Tupac Shakur passed away in Las Vegas, Nevada, he would have been 55 this year.

angles.name: Hip-Hop Legend
angles.copy: Remembering the profound impact of Tupac Shakur, who we lost on this day in 1996. His music continues to resonate decades later.

events.title: Remembering Jimi Hendrix
events.blurb: On this day in 1970, the legendary guitarist Jimi Hendrix passed away in London, England, he would have been 84 this year.

angles.name: Guitar Genius
angles.copy: Today marks the anniversary of losing a true icon, Jimi Hendrix, in 1970. His innovative guitar work redefined rock music forever.

events.title: The Beatles released 'Abbey Road'
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic eleventh studio album, 'Abbey Road', on this day in 1969 in the UK.

angles.name: Iconic Album
angles.copy: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' hit shelves on this day in 1969. The album cover alone is legendary, let alone the tracks inside. What's your favourite?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, lead singer of The Pretenders, was born on this day in 1951 in Akron, Ohio, USA.

angles.name: Rock Rebel
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to the incredible Chrissie Hynde! Her distinctive voice and songwriting have made The Pretenders timeless.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: Liam Gallagher, former lead singer of Oasis and Beady Eye, was born on this day in 1972 in Burnage, Manchester, England.

angles.name: Mad For It
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! What's your favourite tune featuring that unmistakable voice?

events.title: John Lennon released 'Imagine'
events.blurb: John Lennon's iconic single 'Imagine' was released in the US on this day in 1971.

angles.name: Timeless Message
angles.copy: 55 years ago, John Lennon gave the world 'Imagine'. A song that continues to inspire and resonate with its message of peace.

events.title: The first iPod was released
events.blurb: Apple unveiled the first generation iPod on this day in 2001, revolutionising how people listened to music.

angles.name: Music In Your Pocket
angles.copy: Remember life before the iPod? On this day in 2001, Apple introduced the device that put '1,000 songs in your pocket'. Game changer!

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: James Bond Day is celebrated annually on October 5th, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first 007 film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: Shaken, Not Stirred
angles.copy: It's James Bond Day! Celebrating 007's big screen debut with 'Dr. No' in 1962. Who's your favourite Bond and what's the best theme tune?

events.title: Remembering John Lennon
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, co-founder of The Beatles, was born on this day in 1940 in Liverpool, England, and would have been 86 this year.

angles.name: Liverpool Legend
angles.copy: Celebrating the life and music of John Lennon, who would have turned 86 today. His legacy of peace and iconic songs live on.

events.title: John Cleese turns 87
events.blurb: English actor, comedian, screenwriter, and Monty Python co-founder John Cleese was born on this day in 1939 in Weston-super-Mare, Somerset, England.

angles.name: Comedy Genius
angles.copy: The legendary John Cleese celebrates his 87th birthday today! From Monty Python to Fawlty Towers, a true master of British comedy.

events.title: The Beatles released 'The White Album'
events.blurb: The Beatles released their ninth studio album, 'The Beatles', commonly known as 'The White Album', on this day in 1968.

angles.name: Double Album Delight
angles.copy: On this day in 1968, The Beatles unleashed 'The White Album' on the world. A sprawling, diverse masterpiece. What's your standout track?
